data.name: Tip_Add
data.value: Add a server that already exists in a folder

data.name: EmptyState
data.value: Select or add a server to get started.

data.name: Tip_ChangeIcon
data.value: Click to change the server icon (what players see)

data.name: Tunnel_Placeholder
data.value: Auto-detected, or paste the tunnel address here...

data.name: Tip_CreateTunnel
data.value: Create the Playit tunnel for this server (by its port)

data.name: Tip_Web
data.value: Open the Playit.gg panel to create or view tunnels

data.name: Tip_Service
data.value: Start/stop the Playit service (may require admin)

data.name: Command_Placeholder
data.value: Type a command (e.g. say hi, op Alex, stop) and press Enter...

data.name: Tip_Commands
data.value: See available commands and what they do

data.name: Players_ConnectedHint
data.value: Actions available when the server is running.

data.name: Whitelist_Hint
data.value: Only these players can join (if the whitelist is enabled in Configure).

data.name: RestartNeeded
data.value: The language will apply after restarting the app. Restart now?

data.name: Cs_UsePlayit
data.value: Use Playit.gg for this server (Internet tunnel)

data.name: Cs_AutoCreateTunnel
data.value: Automatically create the Playit.gg tunnel for this server

data.name: Cs_AutoStart
data.value: Start the server when done to generate the world and files

data.name: Cfg_HeaderFmt
data.value: Settings for "{0}". Changes apply when you (re)start the server.

data.name: Cfg_MotdDesc
data.value: The text players see under the server name in the Minecraft list. Supports color codes with § (e.g. §a green, §6 gold, §l bold).

data.name: Cfg_GamemodeDesc
data.value: Default mode for players who join: Survival (health/hunger), Creative (unlimited resources and flight), Adventure (can't break blocks) or Spectator.

data.name: Cfg_DifficultyDesc
data.value: How much damage enemies and hunger do. Peaceful spawns no monsters and doesn't drain hunger; Hard hits harder and you can even starve to death.

data.name: Cfg_MaxPlayersDesc
data.value: Maximum number of players connected at once. More players need more RAM and CPU.

data.name: Cfg_PvpDesc
data.value: If enabled, players can damage each other. Turn it off for a peaceful/cooperative server.

data.name: Cfg_HardcoreDesc
data.value: Extreme mode: difficulty locked to Hard and, on death, the player is permanently set to spectator. Use only for challenges.

data.name: Cfg_FlightDesc
data.value: In Survival, prevents the server from kicking players who fly (useful with mods/plugins that grant flight). Doesn't grant flight by itself.

data.name: Cfg_CmdBlocksDesc
data.value: Allows command blocks to create automatic mechanics (maps, minigames). Leave it off if you don't use them.

data.name: Cfg_SpawnProtDesc
data.value: Radius (in blocks) around spawn where normal players can't build or break. 0 disables it.

data.name: Cfg_ViewDistDesc
data.value: How far chunks are loaded and sent to players. Higher = see farther, but uses more CPU and network. Typical: 8–12.

data.name: Cfg_SimDistDesc
data.value: How far the server 'simulates' the world (mobs, crops, redstone). Higher = more active things at once, more CPU. Typical: 8–10.

data.name: Cfg_GenStructuresDesc
data.value: Whether villages, dungeons, temples, strongholds, etc. generate in the world. Almost always left on.

data.name: Cfg_PortDesc
data.value: Local port the server listens on. If you change it, remember the Playit tunnel must point to this same port.

data.name: Cfg_OnlineModeDesc
data.value: On: only legitimate Minecraft accounts can join (recommended and safe). Off ('cracked'): anyone can join, with no identity or skin verification.

data.name: Cfg_WhitelistDesc
data.value: If enabled, only players added to the whitelist can join. Ideal for private servers with friends.

data.name: Ae_JavaPlaceholder
data.value: java (uses the one in PATH) or path to java.exe

data.name: Del_ConfirmFmt
data.value: Remove the server "{0}" from the list?

data.name: Del_Tunnel
data.value: Also delete its Playit.gg tunnel (the one pointing to its port).

data.name: Del_Files
data.value: Also delete all server files on disk (including the world).

data.name: Del_Warning
data.value: The entire server folder will be permanently deleted. This action CANNOT be undone.

data.name: Pk_Intro
data.value: To create and delete tunnels automatically, the app needs a Playit key with write permission. The agent key you already have is read-only and won't work for this.

data.name: Pk_Steps
data.value: Generate a key in your playit.gg account (keys / API section, with write permission) and paste it here. It is stored only on your computer.

data.name: Signal_NoTunnel
data.value: On on your PC, but the Playit tunnel is off: nobody can join from the Internet.

data.name: Cs_PortNote
data.value: To run several servers at once, each must use a different port.

data.name: Msg_JavaCompatibleFound
data.value: Compatible Java {0} found. Nothing to install.

data.name: Msg_JavaDownloading
data.value: Downloading Java (this may take a while)...

data.name: Msg_JavaExeNotFound
data.value: Java was downloaded but java.exe was not found.

data.name: Msg_ChecksumMismatchFmt
data.value: The downloaded file ({0}) didn't match the official checksum and was deleted. The download may have been corrupted or tampered with; try again.

data.name: Msg_NoServerDownload
data.value: Version {0} has no downloadable server available.

data.name: Msg_MissingWriteKey
data.value: The Playit write key is missing.

data.name: Msg_PlayitAuthFail
data.value: Could not authenticate with Playit to read the tunnels.

data.name: Msg_PlayerNotFoundMojang
data.value: Player '{0}' was not found on Mojang. Check that the name is spelled correctly.

data.name: Msg_NotRespondingKill
data.value: [Launcher] The server didn't respond in time; forcing it to close.

data.name: Msg_PlayitServiceNotInstalled
data.value: [Playit] The Playit service is not installed on this computer.

data.name: Msg_PlayitServiceChangeFail
data.value: [Playit] Could not change the service (usually needs running the app as administrator, or using the tray icon): {0}

data.name: Msg_PortUnknown
data.value: [Playit] Could not determine the server port (is server.properties missing?).

data.name: Msg_NeedsJavaPreparing
data.value: [Launcher] This server needs Java {0}. Preparing a compatible Java...

data.name: Msg_NeedsJavaCurrentPreparing
data.value: [Launcher] This server needs Java {0} (configured one is Java {1}). Preparing a compatible Java...

data.name: Msg_JavaPrepareFailStart
data.value: [Warning] Could not prepare Java {0}: {1}. Will try to start anyway.

data.name: Msg_PortStillBusy
data.value: [Error] Port {0} is still busy. Try again in a few seconds.

data.name: Msg_CreatingTunnel
data.value: [Playit] Creating Minecraft Java tunnel for port {0}...

data.name: Msg_TunnelCreated
data.value: [Playit] Tunnel created successfully. The address will appear shortly.

data.name: Msg_TunnelExists
data.value: [Playit] A tunnel for port {0} already existed; reusing it.

data.name: Msg_NeedRunningFor
data.value: [Players] The server must be running to {0}.

data.name: Msg_PortBusyConfirm
data.value: Port {0} is already in use by {1}. This usually happens if a previous server got stuck. Do you want to close that process and start the server?

data.name: Msg_NoTunnelForPort
data.value: No Playit tunnel was found for port {0}. No tunnel was deleted.

data.name: Msg_TunnelDeleteError
data.value: Could not delete the Playit tunnel: {0}

data.name: Msg_FilesDeleteError
data.value: The server was removed from the list, but not all files could be deleted: {0}

data.name: Msg_NameRequired
data.value: Enter a name for the server.

data.name: Msg_FolderNotExistCreate
data.value: The folder to create the server in does not exist.

data.name: Msg_RamMaxMin
data.value: Max RAM must be greater than or equal to min RAM.

data.name: Msg_PortAssigned
data.value: Port {0} is already assigned to another registered server. Choose a different port (the button suggests a free one).

data.name: Msg_PortInUseOther
data.value: Port {0} is already in use by another application. Choose a different port.

data.name: Msg_FolderExists
data.value: The folder already exists and is not empty: {0}. Use it anyway?

data.name: Msg_UseSystemJava
data.value: The system 'java' will be used; if it's not compatible, install it manually.

data.name: Msg_WritingEula
data.value: Accepting the EULA and generating run.bat...

data.name: Msg_PortsInUseByServers
data.value: Ports already used by other servers: {0}

data.name: Msg_FolderNotExist
data.value: The server folder does not exist.

data.name: Title_SelectFolderCreate
data.value: Folder to create the server in

data.name: Title_SelectImage
data.value: Choose an image for the server icon

data.name: Msg_ClosingTitle
data.value: MC Server Launcher — stopping servers and saving...

data.name: Whatsnew_1_1_0
data.value: • MC Server Launcher now runs on Linux too (not just Windows), thanks to a new cross-platform interface. • On Linux, download it as an AppImage from the releases; on Windows everything stays the same (installer and auto-update). • All 5 languages, the console, the players view and the visual configuration are kept.

data.name: Whatsnew_1_2_0
data.value: • You can now create Forge and Paper servers, on top of Vanilla and Fabric. • Built-in mods & plugins browser (Modrinth), filtered by your server's type and version, with sorting and one-click install. • Enable/disable or delete what's installed, and convert an existing server to another type while keeping the world. • Each server now shows a type badge.

data.name: Whatsnew_1_2_1
data.value: • More security: the Playit API key is now stored encrypted (previously plain text), on every OS. Migration is automatic. • Refreshed high-resolution app icon.

data.name: Whatsnew_1_2_2
data.value: • More security: Minecraft (Mojang), Java (Adoptium) and Paper downloads are now verified against their official checksum; a mismatch deletes the file and warns instead of using it.

data.name: Whatsnew_1_2_3
data.value: • More security: mods and plugins you install from Modrinth are now also verified against their official checksum before use.

data.name: Cmd_HelpIntro
data.value: Click a command to put it in the box and fill in the details. These commands only work while the server is running.

data.name: Loader_Intro
data.value: Change this server's type: Fabric or Forge for mods, Paper for plugins, or Vanilla for none. Your world is kept.

data.name: Loader_Kept
data.value: The world, server.properties and your mods/plugins and config folders are kept.

data.name: Loader_WarnSame
data.value: You're reinstalling the same server type. It only makes sense to change version; the startup is rewritten and you may lose some settings.

data.name: Loader_WarnVanillaToLoader
data.value: Changing the type of a vanilla server is safe. The world is kept; you'll then be able to install mods or plugins depending on the chosen type.

data.name: Loader_WarnToVanilla
data.value: Warning! Going back to Vanilla from a server with mods or plugins can corrupt or empty parts of the world that use added content (blocks or entities). Make a backup. Switching back to the previous type usually recovers it.

data.name: Loader_WarnCrossLoader
data.value: Danger! Switching between types with incompatible content (from one mod loader to another, or between mods and Paper plugins) can break the world: added content from one doesn't exist in the other. Back up first. Returning to the previous type usually recovers it.

data.name: Msg_ForgeInstallerTimeout
data.value: The Forge installer took too long and was cancelled. Check your connection and try again.

data.name: Filter_Tip
data.value: Only results compatible with this server type and Minecraft version are shown.

data.name: Msg_PaperNoBuild
data.value: No Paper build is available for Minecraft {0}.

data.name: HowToPlay_PaperFmt
data.value: This server uses Paper (Minecraft {0}). Plugins live only on the server: 1. Players just join with normal Minecraft (version {0}); they install nothing. 2. Plugins (.jar) go in the server's plugins folder. 3. Restart the server after adding or removing plugins.

data.name: Loader_Warning
data.value: The chosen loader will be applied with that version. Use the same version the server already had to avoid breaking the world.

data.name: Loader_Done
data.value: Done. The server has been updated.

data.name: Msg_ModBrowserNeedsLoader
data.value: The mod browser is only available on Fabric or Forge servers.

data.name: Msg_NoModsFound
data.value: No mods found for this version and server type.

data.name: Msg_ForgeNoVersion
data.value: No Forge version is available for Minecraft {0}.

data.name: Msg_ForgeRunningInstaller
data.value: Running the Forge installer (this can take a while)...

data.name: Msg_ForgeInstallNoOutput
data.value: The Forge installer did not produce a recognizable server.

data.name: HowToPlay_Title
data.value: How to play on this server

data.name: HowToPlay_FabricFmt
data.value: This server uses Fabric (Minecraft {0}). To join with mods: 1. Install the Fabric client from https://fabricmc.net/use (pick version {0}). 2. Open the Minecraft launcher and select the "fabric-loader-{0}" profile. 3. Copy the mods (.jar) into %appdata%\.minecraft\mods. 4. The client mod versions must match the server's.

data.name: HowToPlay_ForgeFmt
data.value: This server uses Forge (Minecraft {0}). To join with mods: 1. Download the Forge installer for {0} from https://files.minecraftforge.net and install the client ("Install client"). 2. Open the Minecraft launcher and select the "forge" profile. 3. Copy the mods (.jar) into %appdata%\.minecraft\mods. 4. The client Forge version must match the server's.

data.name: Export_InstructionsFmt
Cmd_Say_Desc
data.value: Sends a chat message to all players.

data.name: Cmd_List_Desc
data.value: Shows the players connected right now.

data.name: Cmd_Op_Desc
data.value: Gives operator (admin) permissions to a player.

data.name: Cmd_Deop_Desc
data.value: Removes operator permissions from a player.

data.name: Cmd_Ban_Desc
data.value: Bans a player so they cannot join.

data.name: Cmd_WlAdd_Desc
data.value: Adds a player to the whitelist.

data.name: Cmd_WlRemove_Desc
data.value: Removes a player from the whitelist.

data.name: Cmd_Gamemode_Desc
data.value: Changes the game mode (survival, creative, adventure, spectator).

data.name: Cmd_Tp_Desc
data.value: Teleports a player to another player or coordinates.

data.name: Cmd_Difficulty_Desc
data.value: Changes the difficulty (peaceful, easy, normal, hard).

data.name: Cmd_Gamerule_Desc
data.value: Changes a game rule (e.g. keepInventory true).

data.name: Cmd_SaveAll_Desc
data.value: Saves the world to disk immediately.

data.name: Cmd_Stop_Desc
data.value: Shuts down the server safely (saving the world).
